title: Fourteen Holy Helpers Novena
description: Invoke Heaven’s emergency response team. Pray to the Fourteen Holy Helpers now for protection from plague, disease, and every affliction that threatens you.

title: 30 Day Novena to St Joseph
description: Seek St. Joseph’s powerful intercession today. Pray this 30-day novena and receive the protection of Jesus’ foster father, patron of impossible causes.

title: The 54 Day Rosary Novena
description: Pray the most powerful Marian devotion now. Experience miracles through this 54-day Rosary Novena - the spiritual weapon Our Lady gave us for every need.

title: All Saints Novena
description: Unite with Heaven’s greatest heroes today. Pray this novena now and invoke the combined intercession of countless saints who reign with Christ.

title: Angelic Warfare Confraternity Novena
description: Conquer temptation and reclaim purity. Pray now for the angelic protection promised by St. Thomas Aquinas. Experience true freedom in body and soul.

title: Annunciation Novena
description: Unite with Mary’s ‘yes’ to God. Pray the Annunciation Novena today and open your heart to receive God’s miraculous plan for your life.

title: Assumption Novena
description: Prepare for eternal life with Mary. Pray the Assumption Novena now and receive the grace of a holy death. Honor the Queen crowned body and soul in Heaven.

title: Bl Fr Michael McGivney Novena
description: Blessed Fr. Michael McGivney was an Irish-American priest who founded the Knights of Columbus to serve immigrants and their families.

title: Bl Miguel Agustin Pro Novena
description: Blessed Miguel Agustin Pro was a Mexican Jesuit priest executed under Plutarco Elías Calles on false charges. Pray this novena for those who labor, those who are sick, and for those suffering from depression.

title: Blessed Francis Xavier Seelos Novena
description: Seek miraculous healing and divine guidance today. Pray to Blessed Francis Xavier Seelos, the cheerful saint who brought hope to countless suffering souls.

title: Blessed Solanus Casey Novena
description: Blessed Solanus Casey was an American Capuchin Franciscan priest who demonstrated great humility and love for others in spite of many setbacks and obstacles he faced throughout his life. Pray this novena for overcoming setbacks and apparent failures.

title: Blessed Stanley Rother Novena
description: Find courage to stand firm in faith. Pray to Blessed Stanley Rother, the American martyr who laid down his life for Christ’s flock. Pray this novena now.

title: Christ the King Novena
description: Proclaim Christ’s reign over all creation. Pray this novena now and experience the peace that comes from surrendering to the King of Kings and Lord of Lords.

title: Corpus Christi Novena
description: Deepen your love for the Eucharist. Pray this Corpus Christi novena now and experience transforming devotion to Christ’s Real Presence - Body, Blood, Soul, and Divinity.

title: Divine Mercy Novena
description: Trust in Jesus’ infinite mercy today. Pray the Divine Mercy Novena Jesus gave to St. Faustina and experience the ocean of grace poured out for all souls.

title: Election Novena
description: Pray for God’s will in our nation. Join this Election Novena today and intercede for truth, justice, and righteous leadership in America and beyond.

title: Consecration of the Election to the Blessed Virgin Mary Novena
description: Pray for God’s will in our nation. Join this Election Novena today and intercede for truth, justice, and righteous leadership in America and beyond.

title: English Martyrs Novena
description: Find courage in persecution. Pray to the English Martyrs now for unshakeable faith and the strength to witness boldly to Christ in hostile times.

title: Novena to the Magi in Anticipation of the Epiphany
description: Journey with the Wise Men to meet Christ. Pray this Epiphany novena now and prepare your heart to offer Him the precious gifts of your life.

title: Venerable Fulton Sheen Novena
description: Venerable Fulton John Sheen was an American bishop who utilized television and radio to evangelize and catechize. Pray this novena for the beatification and canonization of Fulton Sheen.

title: Guardian Angel Novena
description: God gives every person a Guardian Angel, who watches over them from their conception until death. Pray this novena to invoke the aid of your Guardian Angel.

title: Holy Cloak of St. Joseph Novena 30 Days
description: St. Joseph was the husband of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the foster-father of Jesus Christ. Pray the novena to St. Joseph for finding employment, selling your house, finding your spouse, or any intention.

title: Holy Cross Novena
description: The Exaltation of the Holy Cross celebrates the discovery by St Helena of the True Cross of Christ. Pray this Holy Cross Novena to draw closer to Christ Crucified.

title: Holy Face of Jesus Novena
description: The Holy Face of Jesus Novena was given by Jesus to Sister Marie de Saint-Pierre, a Carmelite nun from Tours, France, to make reparation for the evils of atheists, Masons, and Catholics who did not observe Sunday properly.

title: Holy Family Novena

feastRule.text: The feast is celebrated on the Sunday within the Octave of Christmas, that is, the Sunday between Christmas Day and New Year's Day (both exclusive), or if both Christmas Day and the Solemnity of Mary, Mother of God are Sundays, on 30 December (always a Friday in such years).

description: The Holy Family of Jesus, Mary, and Joseph provides every family an inspiration and model to follow.

title: Holy Innocents Novena
description: The Holy Innocents were the male children two years old and younger that lived near Bethlehem whom King Herod ordered to be slaughtered. Pray this novena for foundlings, babies, and children’s choirs.

title: Holy Name of Jesus Novena
description: Since the time of the Apostles, the Church has reverenced the Holy Name of Jesus. Philippians 2:10 says Pray this novena for greater devotion to the Holy Name of Jesus.

title: Novena for the Holy Souls in Purgatory
description: St. Alphonsus Liguori composed this novena of prayers for the Holy Souls in Purgatory, to be prayed leading up to All Souls Day

title: Immaculate Conception Novena
description: Our Lady appeared to St. Bernadette in Lourdes, France and confirmed that she was “the Immaculate Conception.”

title: Immaculate Heart of Mary Novena
description: Devotion to the Immaculate Heart of Mary springs from the Church’s reflection on her life, especially in relation to her Son, Jesus Christ. Pray this novena for greater devotion to Our Lady and Her Son.

title: Annunciation Impossible Requests 9 Month Novena
description: Unite with Mary’s ‘yes’ to God. Pray the Annunciation Novena today and open your heart to receive God’s miraculous plan for your life.

title: Infant of Prague Novena
description: This devotion comes from a lovely statue of the child Jesus, said to have once belonged to St. Teresa of Avila, holding a globe with a cross on top, representing Christ’s dominion over the whole world.

title: Mary, Help of Christians Novena
description: Mary, Help of Christians is one of the most ancient titles of the Blessed Virgin Mary. Pray this novena for Our Lady’s help in battling against sin and evil.

title: Mary, Queen of the Apostles Novena

startRule.text: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Easter

description: The title of Mary, Queen of the Apostles, is an ancient title of Our Lady in the Church. Pray this novena for consolation, more fruitful prayers, deeper conversion, and for an outpouring of the Holy Spirit.

title: Mary Undoer of Knots Novena - Unfailing Novena Prayer
description: Mary Undoer of Knots is a title of the Virgin Mary where she unties the knots in our lives. Pray the Unfailing Novena to Mary Undoer of Knots.

title: Miraculous Medal Novena
description: The Miraculous Medal was given to Daughter of Charity, St Catherine Labouré, by the Virgin Mary through a series of three apparitions. Pray this novena to receive graces from the Miraculous Medal devotion.

title: Nativity of Mary Novena
description: The Nativity of Mary celebrates the birthday of the Blessed Virgin Mary. Pray this novena for Malta, Cuba, Philippines, Kerala India, and for greater devotion to Our Lady.

title: North American Martyrs Novena
description: The North American Martyrs (also known as the Canadian Martyrs) were French Jesuit missionaries to the Native Americans who were martyred for their faith. Pray this novena for Canada and North America.

title: Novena for Fertility to Conceive a Baby
description: The Novena for Fertility to Conceive a Child invokes several saints known for their intercession against infertility. Pray this novena to conceive a baby, to be healed of infertility, and to become pregnant.

title: Novena for the Repose of Pope Francis and for the New Pope
tags: Novena for Pope's Repose and New Pope
description: Upon the death of the pope, the Church observes a period of mourning for nine days, which includes the pope’s funeral. The Church then begins a conclave to elect a new pope. Pray this novena for the repose of the soul of Pope Francis and for the election of the new pope.

title: Novena to the Divine Infant King
tags: Novena to the Divine Infant King
description: Embrace childlike holiness today. Pray to the Divine Infant King now and receive the gifts of humility, innocence, meekness, joy, and obedience to God’s will.

title: Holy Spirit Novena - Powerful Pentecost Prayers
description: The Holy Spirit Novena for the Seven Gifts is traditionally prayed leading up to Pentecost to celebrate the descent of the Holy Spirit upon Mary and the Apostles.

title: Our Lady of Champion Novena
description: Our Lady of Champion is an apparition of Our Lady to Belgian immigrant Adele Brise in Wisconsin in 1859. Pray this novena for Our Lady’s help and the conversion of sinners.

title: Our Lady of Charity Novena
description: Our Lady of Charity, known also as La Vírgen de la Caridad del Cobre, is a title and image of Our Lady in Cuba. Pray this novena for Cuba, Cuban peoples, salt and copper miners.

title: Our Lady of Czestochowa Novena
description: Our Lady of Czestochowa, also known as The Black Madonna of Częstochowa, is a miraculous icon of the Virgin Mary, located in the Jasna Góra Monastery in Poland, which has been venerated by many popes and countless Catholics. She is the patroness of Poland.

title: Our Lady of Fatima Novena
description: The Blessed Virgin Mary appeared in 1917 to three children in the little village of Fatima, Portugal. She told them to pray the Rosary for peace, for sinners, for World War I to end, and for the conversion of Russia.

title: Our Lady of Good Counsel Novena
description: Our Lady of Good Counsel is one of the titles of the Blessed Virgin Mary, named after a painting miraculously rescued from Albania during the Turkish invasion.

title: Our Lady of Good Health Novena
description: Our Lady of Good Health appeared to two boys in India the 16th century and saved a Portuguese merchant ship in the 17th century in India, where a chapel was then built in her honor. Pray this novena for healing and protection of health.

title: Our Lady of Good Success Novena
description: The Blessed Virgin Mary and Jesus appeared in 1610 to Venerable Mother Mariana de Jesus Torres in Quito, Ecuador, giving prophecies of a coming crisis in the Church and of several infallible papal dogmatic decrees.

title: Our Lady of Graces Novena
description: Our Lady of Graces is a title of the Virgin Mary given in gratitude for graces received through her intercession. Pray this novena for the Diocese of Faenza, cities of Minturno, Cautano, Decimoputzu, Nettuno, Ricadi, Sanluri, Toritto, and for graces needed.

title: Our Lady of Guadalupe Novena
description: Our Lady appeared in the year 1531 to Juan Diego, a simple peasant of Aztec descent who had converted to Catholicism.

title: Our Lady of Hope Novena
description: Our Lady of Hope, also known as Our Lady of Portmain, commerorates an apparition of the Virgin Mary during January of 1871 to several children during the Franco-Prussian war.

title: Our Lady of Knock Novena
description: On August 21st, 1879 Mary McLoughlin and a group of other Irish Catholics were given a vision of the Virgin Mary, St Joseph, and St John the Evangelist. Pray this novena in honor of Our Lady of Knock for your intentions.

title: Our Lady of La Leche Novena
description: Our Lady of La Leche is title of the Blessed Virgin Mary originating from an ancient statue of her breastfeeding the infant Jesus. Pray this novena to conceive a child and against infertility, for a safe and healthy delivery of your baby, for motherhood, for Florida, and for nursing your baby.

title: Our Lady of La Salette Novena
description: Our Lady of La Salette appeared to two young shephereds in the French Alps in 1846. Pray this novena for La Salette-Fallavaux, Silang, and Cavite.

title: Our Lady of Lourdes Novena
description: Our Lady appeared to St. Bernadette Soubirous eighteen times in Lourdes, France in 1858, just four years after the Pope had declared the Immaculate Conception to be dogma.

title: Our Lady of Monte Berico Novena
description: Our Lady of Monte Berico ended a plague in Italy in the 1420s. Pray this novena for an end to pandemics.

title: Our Lady of Mt. Carmel Novena
description: Mary appeared to St. Simon Stock, an early Carmelite, and gave him a brown scapular, representing part of her own garment as a special protection and calls the wearers to consecrate themselves to her in a special way.

title: Our Lady of Peace Novena
description: Our Lady of Peace is a traditional title of the Virgin Mary. Pray this novena for peace, Congregation of the Sacred Hearts of Jesus and Mary, El Salvador, Maine, Hawaii.

title: Our Lady of Perpetual Help Novena
description: A Byzantine icon from the 1400s depicts Our Lady of Perpetual Help, holding the child Jesus tenderly as He looks at the implements of His Passion.

title: Our Lady of Sorrows Novena
description: We call to mind in this novena the sorrows that our Blessed Mother endured during her life. We live in a valley of tears, until we reach our heavenly homeland, and so we ask our Lady to intercede for us.

title: Our Lady of the Rosary Novena
description: Our Lady of the Rosary (originally Our Lady of Victory) celebrates the miraculous naval defeat of the Turkish Islamic invaders in 1571 at the Battle of Lepanto.

title: Our Lady of the Snows Novena
description: Our Lady of the Snows commemorates an apparition of Our Lady to a faithful husband and wife in Rome in the 300s where she made snow to fall during the hot month of August. Pray this novena for increased dedication to Our Lady.

title: Our Lady of Walsingham Novena
description: Our Lady of Walsingham is one of England’s most significant Marian shrines, with origins tracing back to the 11th century. Pray this novena for the Ordinariate, England, the protection of families, and the reconversion of England to the Catholic Faith.

title: Our Lady Star of the Sea Novena
tags: Our Lady Star of the Sea
description: Our Lady, Star of the Sea is a title given to the Virgin Mary originating in a translation by St. Jerome. Pray this novena for sailors, coastal communities, those in need of guidance, and travelers.

title: Pope St. John Paul II Novena
description: Pope St. John Paul II grew up in Poland, endured the horrors of war and Nazi occupation, then rose to become a beloved pope of the late 20th century.

title: Pope St Leo the Great Novena
description: Pope St Leo the Great (Pope Leo I) was a fifth century pope who combated the heresies of Pelagianism, Manichaeism, and Monophysitism. Pray this novena for unity and orthodoxy in the Church.

title: Precious Blood Novena
description: The Blood of Jesus, shed for our salvation, has long been a focal point of the Church’s devotion. Pray this novena to grow in devotion to Christ and His Precious Blood.

title: Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary Novena
tags: Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
description: Sts Joachim and Ann presented their daughter, the Blessed Virgin Mary, in the Temple in Jerusalem when she was a little girl, consecrating her to God. Pray this novena for consecrating your children to God through Our Lady’s intercession.

title: Queenship of Mary Novena
description: The Feast of the Queenship of Mary honors the Blessed Virgin Mary as Queen of Heaven and Earth. Pray this novena in honor of Mary as our Queen.

title: Respect Life Novena
description: January is the month the Church prays in a special way for the protection of human life, especially for unborn children, in the novena for life.

title: Novena to the Sacred Heart of Jesus
description: St. Margaret Mary Alacoque was given this devotion by Jesus to His Sacred Heart. It was also prayed everyday by St. Padre Pio.

title: Seven Sorrows and Joys of St Joseph Novena
tags: Seven Sorrows and Joys of St Joseph
description: The seven sorrows and joys of St. Joseph are an old devotion that meditates on the sufferings of St. Joseph and the corresponding joys of his life. Pray this novena to grow in devotion to St. Joseph, our Lady, and Jesus and to overcome all anxiety and fear.

title: St Abigail Novena
description: St Abigail (also called St Gobnait or St Deborah) was a sixth century Irish saint. She is the patron saint of honeybees, beekeepers, and ironworkers.

title: St Adelaide Novena
description: St Adelaide of Italy, also known as St Adelaide of Burgundy, was Holy Roman Empress to Emperor Otto the Great during the tenth century. Pray this novena for abuse victims, brides, empresses, exiles, in-law problems, parenthood, parents of large families, princesses, prisoners, second marriages, step-parents, and widows.

title: St. Agatha Novena
description: St Agatha of Sicily was a beautiful young woman who consecrated her virginity to Christ and was martyred for it. Pray this novena for those suffering from breast cancer, and for nurses.

title: St. Agnes Novena
description: Virgin and martyr, St. Agnes was born around 290 AD to a faithful Catholic family.

title: St Albert the Great Novena
description: St Albert the Great was a German Dominican friar, philosopher, scientist, and bishop who lived in the 1200s. Pray this novena for exams, finals, students, and scientists.

title: St. Aloysius Gonzaga Novena
description: St Aloysius Gonzaga was a sixteenth century Jesuit priest. Pray this novena for students, young people, the blind, purity, plague victims, and against epidemics.

title: St Alphonsus Liguori Novena
description: St Alphonsus Liguori was an Italian bishop, writer, musician, and philosopher who founded the Redemptorists and became a Doctor of the Church. Pray this novena for arthritis, lawyers, confessors, lay apostolates, and vocations.

title: St Ambrose Novena
description: St Ambrose of Milan was a fourth century theologian, statesman, and Bishop of Milan. Pray this novena for beekeepers, beggars, learners, Milan, geese, candlemakers, schoolchildren, and domestic animals.

title: St. Andre Bessette Novena
description: St Andre Bessette was the unlikeliest man to become a saint. He was physically weak, wasn’t a brilliant theologian, and multiple times he was almost expelled from the religious Order that he wanted to be in.

title: St Andrew Avellino Novena
description: Saint Andrew Avellino was an Italian Theatine priest who became a significant figure in the Counter-Reformation against Protestant heresies. Pray this novena for Naples, Sicily, Badalato, and against a sudden death.

title: St. Andrew Christmas Novena
description: The St. Andrew Christmas novena is traditionally recited 15 times per day starting on November 30th and continuing through December 24th.

title: St. Andrew the Apostle Novena
description: St. Andrew the Apostle was the first disciple called by Jesus and brother of St. Peter. He was martyred on an X-shaped cross.

title: St Angela Merici Novena
description: St Angela Merici was an Italian saint who founded the Ursulines and dedicated her life to educating girls. Pray this novena for sickness, handicapped people, loss of parents, courage, strength, and determination.

title: St. Anne Novena - Prayers for Finding Your Husband
description: St. Anne is the mother of the Virgin Mary and grandmother of Jesus, and as such has been accorded great honor within Catholic Tradition.

title: St. Anthony Novena
description: St. Anthony of Padua was a Franciscan friar, known as a gifted preacher and for his closeness to Christ. Pray the St. Anthony Novena for lost things.

title: St Anthony Mary Claret Novena
description: St Anthony Mary Claret was a 19th century Spanish Catholic archbishop and missionary. Pray this novena for textile merchants, weavers, prudentially saving money, Catholic press, Claretians Missionary Sons of the Immaculate Heart of Mary, Diocese of the Canary Islands, Claretian Students, Educators, and Technical and Vocational Educators.

title: St. Augustine Novena
description: St Augustine of Hippo was a priest and bishop, a Doctor of the Church, who converted from a life of dissolution and vice to become one of the greatest theologians in the Church’s history. Pray this novena for overcoming sin and growing in virtue.

title: St. Benedict Novena
description: St Benedict of Nursia was born in Umbria, Italy around AD 480. He became the father of Western Monasticism and founder of the Benedictine Order, who motto is ora et labora–prayer and work. Pray this novena for students and for Europe.

title: St Benedict Joseph Labre Novena
description: St Benedict Joseph Labre, the Beggar Saint, was an 18th century French Franciscan tertiary. Pray this novena for unmarried men, rejects, mental illness, insanity, beggars, the homeless.

title: St Bernadette of Lourdes Novena
description: St Bernadette of Lourdes was young French girl to whom the Virgin Mary appeared under the title of The Immaculate Conception. Pray this novena for illness, people ridiculed for their piety, poverty, shepherds, shepherdesses, and Lourdes, France.

title: St Bernadine of Siena Novena
description: St Bernardine of Siena was a fifteenth century Italian Franciscan friar and preacher renowned for his eloquent preaching and his efforts to reform the clergy and the faithful. Pray this novena for advertisers, chest problems, Italy, gambling addicts, for peace and reconciliation, and public relations personnel.

title: St Bernard of Clairvaux Novena
description: St Bernard of Clairvaux was a Cistercian abbot, mystic, and co-founder of the Knights Templar. Pray this novena for Cistercians, Burgundy, beekeepers, candlemakers, Gibraltar, Algeciras, Queens’ College Cambridge, Knights Templar.

title: St. Blaise Novena
description: St Blaise was bishop of Sebastea in the third and fourth centuries. Pray this novena for throat illnesses.

title: St Brendan Novena
description: St Brendan the Navigator (also called St Brendan of Clonfert) was an Irish monk and considered one of the Twelve Apostles of Ireland. He is the patron saint of boatmen, divers, mariners, sailors, travellers, whales, portaging canoes, Diocese of Clonfert, Diocese of Kerry.

title: St. Bridget of Sweden Novena
description: St Bridget of Sweden, born Birgitta, was a Swedish mystic and religious Order foundress who lived in the 1300s and was gifted with special visions from Jesus Christ.

title: St. Brigid of Ireland Novena
description: Saint Brigid of Ireland (also known as St Brigid of Kildare) was an early Christian nun in Ireland and an abbess over several monasteries. Pray this novena for children in difficult homes, for midwives, farmers, and ranchers.

title: St Cajetan Novena - Prayers for Employment
description: St Cajetan was an Italian Catholic priest and religious reformer, co-founder of the Theatines. He is the patron saint of the unemployed.

title: St Carlo Acutis Novena
description: Pray to the millennial saint today. Blessed Carlo Acutis intercedes for tech workers, gamers, and youth. Experience his joyful devotion to the Eucharist.

title: St Catherine of Alexandria Novena
description: St. Catherine of Alexandria is a virgin saint and martyr in the early Church. Pray this novena for students, unmarried girls, apologists, mechanics, nurses, potters, spinners, archivists, lacemakers, knife sharpeners, philosophers, and librarians.

title: St. Catherine of Siena Novena
description: St Catherine of Siena was a Third Order Dominican who lived in the 1300s. She successfully encouraged the Pope to return to Rome from Avignon and helped the sick during the Black Death in Europe. Pray this novena against fires, sickness, and miscarriages.

title: St. Cecilia Novena
description: St. Cecilia is the patroness of musicians.

title: St Charbel Novena
description: St Charbel Makhlouf was a Lebanese Maronite monk known widely for his holiness and miraculous healings in answer to prayers at his tomb. Pray this novena for the grace to discern a call to the monastic or cloistered religious life, and for miracles you hope for.

title: St Charles Borromeo Novena
description: St Charles Borromeo was an Archbishop and Cardinal who led the Church in the Counter-Reformation against the Protestant Revolt. Pray this novena for bishops, catechists, catechumens, seminarians, spiritual directors, apple orchards, and against ulcers, colic, and intestinal disorders.

title: St Christopher Novena
description: Saint Christopher (Christ-bearer) is a Catholic martyr who once carried Jesus across a raging river. St Christopher is one of the 14 Holy Helpers and is the patron saint of safe travels.

title: St. Clare of Assisi Novena
description: St. Clare grew up in Assisi and from early in her life was devoted to Christ and His Church.

title: St Colette Novena
description: St. Colette (Saint Colette of Corbie), was a French abbess who founded the Colettine Poor Clares, a reform branch of the Order of Saint Clare. St Colette is the patron saint of women hoping to conceive, expectant mothers, and sick children.

title: St Corona Novena to end the Coronavirus Pandemic
description: St Corona lived in the second century, was martyred with St. Victor in 170 AD, and is one of the patron saints of plagues and epidemics. Pray this novena to end an epidemic.

title: St Cyril of Jerusalem Novena
description: St Cyril of Jerusalem was priest then bishop of the Church in the 300s. Pray this novena for catechists and catechumens wanting to enter the Church.

title: St Damien of Molokai Novena
description: St. Damien of Molokai, born Jozef De Veuster on January 3, 1840, in Tremelo, Belgium, is renowned for his compassionate ministry to people with leprosy (Hansen’s disease) on the Hawaiian island of Molokai. Pray this novena for those with leprosy and for outcasts.

title: St. Dominic Novena
description: Saint Dominic was a Castilian priest and founder of the Dominican Order. Pray this novena for greater eloquence or for defense against being falsely accused, as well as for greater devotion to Our Lady and the Rosary.

title: St. Dominic Savio Novena
description: St Dominic Savio was a heroically devout Italian altar boy who studied under St John Bosco and died at the age of 14.

title: St Dymphna Novena - Prayers for Mental Illnesses
description: St. Dymphna was born in Ireland to a non-Christian father, who became mentally disturbed sought to marry his own daughter. She fled, but he eventually found her and killed her. Pray this novena for help with mental afflictions.

title: St Edith Stein Novena
description: Saint Edith Stein (Saint Teresa Benedicta of the Cross) was a German Jewish philosopher who converted to the Catholic Faith and became a Discalced Carmelite nun. Pray this novena for Europe, loss of parents, converted Jews, martyrs, and World Youth Day.

title: St Edmund Campion Novena
description: Edmund Campion was a 16th century English Jesuit priest and martyr. Pray this novena for the United Kingdom and for reverts to the Faith.

title: St Elizabeth Novena
description: St Elizabeth was the mother of St John the Baptist, the wife of St Zechariah, and the cousin of the Blessed Virgin Mary. Pray this novena for pregnant women and to conceive a baby.

title: St. Elizabeth Ann Seton Novena
description: St Elizabeth Ann Seton was a Catholic convert from Episcopal Protestantism and the first native-born American saint in the Church. Pray this novena for in-law problems, against the death of children or parents, and for widows.

title: St. Elizabeth of Hungary Novena
description: St Elizabeth of Hungary was a thirteenth century royal of Hungary who married, had children, and devoted herself to God and service of the poor. Pray this novena for bakers, beggars, brides, charities, death of children, homeless people, hospitals, Sisters of Mercy, and widows.

title: St Elizabeth of Portugal Novena
description: Saint Elizabeth (Isabel) of Portugal, also known as Elizabeth of Aragon, was a Spanish princess who became Queen of Portugal. She is the patorness of the Third Order of St. Francis and for the Diocese of San Cristóbal de La Laguna.

title: St. Elizabeth of the Trinity Novena
description: St Elizabeth of the Trinity was a Discalced Carmelite nun who followed in the footsteps on St Therese of Lisieux. Pray this novena for sick people, against bodily ills, against the death of parents, and against illness.

title: St. Expeditus Novena
description: St Expeditus (also known as St Expedite), was a Roman centurion and Catholic convert in Armenia martyred in April, 303. He is the patron saint of urgent causes, emergencies, lawsuits, expeditious solutions, against procrastination, merchants, navigators, sailors, students, programmers, and hackers.

title: St. Faustina Novena
description: St Maria Faustina Kowalska was a Polish nun who lived in the early twentieth century and to whom Christ gave the mission to spread devotion to His Divine Mercy.

title: St Frances of Rome Novena
description: St Frances of Rome was an Italian wife and mother, mystic, and Benedictine oblate. Pray this novena for widows, automobile drivers, and Benedictine oblates.

title: St Frances Xavier Cabrini Novena
description: St Frances Xavier Cabrini was an Italian-American nun and foundress of the Missionary Sisters of the Sacred Heart of Jesus. She is the patroness of immigrants and hospital administrators.

title: St. Francis de Sales Novena
description: Born to a rich French family near Geneva, Switzerland in 1567, Francis was devout from an early age and blessed with deep intellect.

title: St. Francis of Assisi Novena
description: St. Francis was born around 1181 in Assisi, Italy, to a wealthy merchant family.

title: St. Francis Xavier Novena of Grace
description: This is the Miraculous Novena of Grace to St. Francis Xavier, the great Jesuit missionary to Asia

title: St Gabriel the Archangel Novena
description: St Gabriel is one of the three Archangels, whom God sent as a messenger to the prophet Daniel in the Old Testament and to Zechariah and Our Lady in the New Testament. Pray the St Gabriel novena for greater devotion to Our Lady.

title: St Gabriel of Our Lady of Sorrows Novena
tags: St Gabriel of Our Lady of Sorrows
description: St Gabriel of Our Lady of Sorrows entered the Passionist religious community as a young man after living a fairly worldly life. Pray this novena for young people, students, priests, and Catholic Action.

title: St. Gemma Galgani Novena
description: St Gemma Galgani was an Italian saint and mystic who received the stigmata (the wounds of our Lord). She dedicated herself to God and to prayer and suffered greatly in her short life, dying at age 25. Pray this novena for purity, for healing from back pain, headaches, and the loss of parents.

title: St Genevieve Novena
description: St Genevieve was a faithful French peasant woman whose prayers protected Paris from the invasion of Attila the Hun. Pray this novena for Paris, fever, and disasters.

title: St George Novena
description: Saint George was a soldier in the Praetorian Guard of the Roman army, martyred under Diocletian for refusing to renounce his Catholic Faith.Pray this novena for agricultural workers, farmers, field workers, soldiers, archers, armourers, equestrians, cavalry, saddle makers, chivalry, peacekeeping missions, skin diseases, lepers and leprosy, syphilis, sheep, shepherds, scouting, and England.

title: St Gerard Majella Novena For Expectant Mothers
description: St Gerard Majella, the Mothers’ Saint, was an Italian Redemptorist lay brother whose intercession led to the miraculous healing of a mother near death from childbirth. Pray this novena for expectant mothers and women struggling with infertility.

title: St Germaine Cousin Novena
description: Saint Germaine Cousin (St. Germaine of Pibrac) was a sixteenth century handicapped French farm girl who suffered physical abuse from her step-mother. Pray this novena for abused children, abandoned and disabled people, loss of parents, and the sick.

title: St. Gertrude the Great Prayers for the Souls in Purgatory Novena
description: St. Gertrude the Great was a Benedictine nun and mystic who was given visions from our Lord and powerful prayers to release souls from Purgatory.

title: St Gianna Beretta Molla Novena
description: Gianna Beretta Molla is the patron of mothers, physicians, and unborn children.

title: St Giuseppe Moscati Novena
description: St Giuseppe Moscati was an Italian doctor and professor who brought his faith into the practice of medicine and into science. Pray this novena for bachelors, doctors, physicians, people rejected by Religious Orders, and for biochemistry.

title: St Gregory the Great Novena
description: St Gregory the Great was a medieval Pope in the late 500s. Pray this novena for musicians, singers, students, and teachers.

title: St Helena Novena
description: St Helena discovered the True Cross, and was the mother of Emperor Constantine. Pray this novena for difficult marriages, divorced men and women, converts, and new discoveries.

title: St Hildegard of Bingen Novena
description: St Hildegard of Bingen (1098-1179) was a German Benedictine abbess, mystic, composer, philosopher, physician, and polymath who became one of the most remarkable figures of the medieval period. She is patroness of musicians, writers, and ecology.

title: St Homobonus Novena
description: St Homobonus was a a twelfth century Italian business owner from Cremona. Pray this novena for business people, entrepreneurship, tailors, shoemakers, and clothworkers.

title: St. Ignatius Loyola Novena
description: St Ignatius of Loyola founded the Society of Jesus in the 1500s. Pray this novena for educators and soldiers.

title: St Isidore of Seville Novena
description: St Isidore of Seville was a sixth century Spanish scholar, theologian, and Archbishop of Seville. Pray this novena for students, computers, computer techs, programmers, and the internet.

title: St. Isidore the Farmer Novena
description: St Isidore the Farmer was a humble Spanish farm laborer whose humility led to many miracles from God. He is the patron saint of farmers, peasants, day laborers, agriculture, and brick layers.

title: St. James the Greater Novena
description: St James the Great (also known as St James the Greater) was one of Jesus’ Apostles and the first Apostle to be martyred. Pray this novena for Spain and for pilgrims.

title: St Jane Frances de Chantal Novena
description: St Jane Frances de Chantal was a French Catholic noblewoman who was married, widowed, and then became a nun. Pray this novena for forgotten people, in-law problems, loss of parents, parents separated from children, and widows.

title: St. Joachim Novena
description: Honor Saint Joachim, father of the Blessed Virgin Mary, and ask his intercession for grandparents, parents, and families seeking steadfast faith.

title: St. Joan of Arc Novena
description: St Joan of Arc was a 15th century French peasant girl who was given visions by God to drive the English out of French lands and bring the Dauphin to Reims for his crowning. Pray this novena for France, soldiers, captives, and if she is your patron saint.

title: St. John Bosco Novena
description: St John Bosco was an Italian priest to whom Christ gave the mission to convert the youth of the country to God. Pray this novena for apprentices, editors and publishers, schoolchildren, magicians, and juvenile delinquents.

title: St John of Damascus Novena
description: St John of Damascus (St John Damascene) was a Catholic monk, priest, hymn composer, and apologist. Pray this novena for pharmacists, icon painters, theology students.

title: St John Henry Newman Novena
description: John Henry Newman was a prominent English theologian, philosopher, and cardinal. He is the patron saint of university students and educators.

title: St John Neumann Novena
description: St John Nepomucene Neumann was a Bohemian Catholic Redemptorist priest. He is the patron saint of Catholic education.

title: St John of God Novena
description: St John of God was a Portuguese soldier who dedicated his life to helping the sick. Pray this novena for booksellers, printers, heart patients, hospitals, nurses, the sick, the mentally ill, the dying, and firefighters.

title: St. John of the Cross Novena
description: St John of the Cross, was a 16th century Spanish Carmelite priest who was key to the reforming movement within the Catholic Church that occurred in response to the Protestant Reformation. Pray this novena for an increase in devotion through prayer.

title: St. John the Apostle Novena
description: St John the Apostle was one of Jesus’ closest disciples. Pray this novena for love, loyalty, friendships, authors, booksellers, burn-victims, poison-victims, art-dealers, editors, publishers, scribes, examinations, scholars, and theologians.

title: St. John the Baptist Novena
description: St. John the Baptist is the patron of countless dioceses, cities, and causes.

title: St. John Vianney Novena
description: St. John Vianney is the patron saint of parish priests.

title: St José Luis Sánchez del Río Novena
tags: St Jose Luis Sanchez del Rio
description: St José Luis Sánchez del Río was a Mexican Cristero boy who was executed for refusing to renounce Christ and the Catholic Faith. Pray this novena for persecuted Catholics, children, and adolescents.

title: St. Josemaria Escriva Novena
description: Josemaria Escriva de Balaguer was born Spain in 1902 to a devout family.

title: St. Joseph Novena
description: St. Joseph was the husband of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the foster-father of Jesus Christ. Pray the novena to St. Joseph for finding employment, selling your house, finding your spouse, or any intention.

title: St Joseph Cupertino Novena - Prayer for Tests and Exams
description: St. Joseph was the husband of the Blessed Virgin Mary and the foster-father of Jesus Christ. Pray the novena to St. Joseph for finding employment, selling your house, finding your spouse, or any intention.

title: St Joseph the Worker Novena
description: St Joseph represents the true model of work for Catholics and all people, since he had the supreme honor of working with Jesus Christ in the carpenter shop at Nazareth. Pray this novena for finding a job and for help with your work.

title: St. Josephine Bakhita Novena
description: St. Josephine Bakhita was born in Sudan, then kidnapped and sold into slavery. Pray this novena for Sudan and survivors of human trafficking.

title: St Juan Diego Novena
description: St Juan Diego was a Chichimec native peasant to whom Our Lady of Guadalupe appeared and worked miracles. Pray this novena for all indigenous people of the Americas.

title: St. Jude Novena - Miraculous Prayer for Hopeless Causes
description: St. Jude Thaddeus was one of the Apostles of Jesus Christ. Pray this novena for hopeless causes, desperate situations, and grave illnesses.

title: St Justin Martyr Novena
description: St Justin Martyr was a an early Christian philosopher and martyr. He is the patron saint of philosophers and lecturers.

title: St Kateri Tekakwitha Novena
description: St Kateri Tekakwitha is the first Native American to be canonized a saint. Pray this novena for fostering ecology, for those in exile, and for Native Americans.

title: St. Katharine Drexel Novena
description: St Katharine Drexel was a wealthy American heiress whom God called to become a nun and serve Native and African Americans. Pray this novena for peace between all races of people, and for philanthropists

title: St Lawrence Novena
description: Saint Lawrence was one of the seven deacons of Rome under Pope Sixtus II who were martyred under Emperor Valerian’s persecution in 258 AD. Pray this novena for Rome, students, miners, tanners, chefs, poor, and firefighters.

title: St. Louis de Montfort Novena
description: St Louis de Montfort was a French priest who eloquently preached on devotion to Our Lady and praying the Rosary. Pray this novena for increased devotion to Our Lady and for preachers.

title: St Louis King of France Novena
description: St. Louis IX was King of France from 1226 to 1270. Pray this novena for France, the Third Order of St. Francis, barbers, distillers, and soldiers.

title: St. Lucy Novena
description: St Lucy (Lucia) of Syracuse, whose name means light, was a faithful young woman who dedicated her life to Christ, rejected a pagan suitor who turned her over to the Roman authorities, and was subsequently tortured and martyred. Pray this novena for eye problems.

title: St Luke Novena
description: St Luke the Evangelist wrote one of the four Gospels, along with the Acts of the Apostles. Pray this novena for physicians and surgeons, and to understand Christ’s words better.

title: St Lydwine Novena
description: St Lydwine (also called Lidwina, Lydwid, Lidwid, and Liduina of Schiedam) is a Dutch saint who suffered from chronic pain, likely multiple sclerosis. She is the patron saint of sickness, chronic pain sufferers, chronically ill, ice skaters, and the town of Schiedam.

title: St Margaret Mary Alacoque Novena
description: St Margaret Mary Alacoque was a French Catholic nun and mystic who promoted devotion to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. Pray this novena for those suffering with polio, devotees of the Sacred Heart, and the loss of parents.

title: St Margaret of Castello Novena
description: St Margaret of Castello was an Italian Third Order Dominican who was disabled, yet had great devotion and sanctity. Pray this novena for the disabled, the blind, and the pro-life movement.

title: St Margaret of Cortona Novena
description: St Margaret of Cortona was a Third Order Franciscan who amended her sinful life and became a great saint. Pray this novena for the falsely accused, hoboes, homeless, insane, orphaned, mentally ill, midwives, penitents, single mothers, reformed prostitutes, stepchildren, and tramps.

title: St Margaret of Scotland Novena
description: St Margaret of Scotland was and English princess and Scottish queen who lived in the eleventh century. Pray this novena for Scotland, mothers, large families, learning, and the poor.

title: St. Maria Goretti Novena
description: St. Maria Goretti is the patron saint of chastity, rape victims, girls, youth, teenage girls, poverty, purity and forgiveness.

title: St Marianne Cope Novena
description: St Marianne Cope was a Franciscan nun accepted a request to move to Hawaii and serve the lepers there, and did so for the rest of her life, following in the footsteps of St Damien of Molokai. Pray this novena for lepers, outcasts, those with HIV/AIDS, and Hawaii.

title: St Mark Xi Tianxiang Novena
description: St Mark Xi Tianxiang is a Chinese Catholic martyr from the 1800s who was addicted to opium his entire life. Pray this novena for overcoming drug addiction, alcohol addiction, or habitual sin.

title: St Martin de Porres Novena
description: St Martin de Porres was a mixed-race lay Dominican brother from Peru. Pray this novena for people of mixed race, innkeepers, barbers, public health workers, and those who fight racism.

title: St. Martin of Tours Novena
description: In 371, the city of Tours needed a new bishop and the people chose Martin.

title: St Mary MacKillop Novena
description: St Mary MacKillop, known as St Mary of the Cross, was an Australian religious sister and founder of the Sisters of St Joseph of the Sacred Heart (the Josephites). Pray this novena for Australia, Archdiocese of Brisbane, and for the Knights of the Southern Cross.

title: St. Mary Magdalene Novena
description: St Mary Magdalene was one of Jesus’ closest followers and earliest disciples. Pray this novena for contemplative life, converts, glove makers, hairdressers, penitent sinners, people ridiculed for their piety, perfumeries, pharmacists, sexual temptation, tanners, and women.

title: St Mary Magdalene de Pazzi Novena
description: Saint Mary Magdalene de’ Pazzi was an Italian Carmelite nun who lived during the late 16th and early 17th centuries. Pray this novena for the sick, for overcoming temptation, and to grow in mental prayer.

title: St Matthew Novena
description: St Matthew left his lucrative work as a tax collector when Jesus called him to become an Apostle. Pray this novena for managing money, bankers, and financial wisdom.

title: St Matthias Apostle Novena
description: St. Matthias the Apostle was chosen to replace Judas Iscariot among the Twelve Apostles following Judas’ betrayal and subsequent death. Pray this novena for alcoholics, carpenters, tailors, Great Falls-Billings Montana, Trier, smallpox, hope, perseverance.

title: St. Maximilian Kolbe Novena
description: St Maximilian Kolbe was Polish Franciscan friar, martyr, and saint who gave his life for a fellow prisoner in Auschwitz during World War II. Pray this novena for drug addicts, prisoners, families, and the pro-life movement.

title: St. Michael the Archangel Novena
description: St. Michael is one of the Archangels and is mentioned several times in the Bible.

title: St Michael’s Lent Novena
description: St. Michael’s Lent is a 40-day period of prayer, fasting, and penance observed from the Feast of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary on August 15 to the Feast of the Archangels (St. Michael, St. Gabriel, and St. Raphael) on September 29, excluding Sundays. Pray this novena to grow closer to God, to Our Lady, and to St. Michael.

title: St. Monica Novena
description: St. Monica is the patroness of wives and abuse victims. Her son was St. Augustine.

title: Mother Teresa of Calcutta Novena
description: Mother Teresa was an Albanian Roman Catholic nun and missionary to India. Pray this novena for World Youth Day, the Missionaries of Charity, and for Calcutta Archdiocese.

title: St Nicholas of Myra Novena
description: St Nicholas was a bishop at Myra (Turkey) in the fourth century, renowned for combating the Arian heresy and for giving gifts to children and families in need. Pray this novena for your children.

title: St Odilia Novena
description: Saint Odilia is a virgin martyr from England from the 300s. Pray this novena for good eyesight.

title: St Padre Pio Novena
description: St. Pio of Pietrelcina, or Padre Pio as he is affectionately known by millions, was a Franciscan priest gifted by God in amazing ways.

title: St. Patrick Novena
description: St. Patrick was enslaved as a teenager but escaped and became a missionary to Ireland. His courage and faith converted the Irish from Celtic paganism to Catholicism.

title: St Paul of the Cross Novena
description: St. Paul of the Cross was an Italian mystic, preacher, and founder of the Passionists religious Congregation. Pray this novena for deeper devotion to Christ’s Passion.

title: St Pedro Calungsod Novena
description: St Pedro Calungsod is a seventeenth century Filipino martyr. Pray this novena for Filipino youth, catechumens, altar servers, the Philippines, Overseas Filipino Workers, Guam, Cebuanos, Visayans, Archdiocese of Cebu, Pury, San Antonio, Quezon Province.

title: St. Peregrine Novena
description: St. Peregrine is the patron saint of cancer patients. Pray the St. Peregrine novena for healing.

title: St. Peter and Paul Novena
description: St Peter and St Paul are Apostles and two great pillars of the Catholic Church. St Peter was the first bishop of Rome, and St Paul is considered the greatest missionary evangelist in the Church.

title: St Peter Chanel Novena
description: Saint Peter Chanel was a Catholic priest, missionary, and martyr. Pray this novena for Oceania and for the courage to evangelize.

title: St Peter Julian Eymard Novena
description: St Peter Julian Eymard was a French Catholic priest who founded two religious communities: the Congregation of the Blessed Sacrament for men and the Servants of the Blessed Sacrament for women. Pray this novena for increased devotion to Christ in the Blessed Sacrament.

title: St. Philip Neri Novena
description: St Philip Neri was a Catholic missionary and founder of the Congregation of the Oratory, which includes priests and brothers. Pray this novena for Rome, the U.S. Special Forces, humor and joy, the Philippines, comedians, artists, and writers

title: St. Philomena Novena
description: St. Philomena is the patron saint of infants and youth.

title: St Pier Giorgio Frassati Novena
description: Find radical joy in faith! Pray to St. Pier Giorgio Frassati now. Experience powerful intercession for students, athletes, and young people seeking Christ.

title: St. Raphael the Archangel Novena - Healing Prayers and To Find Spouse
description: St. Raphael is one of the three Archangels mentioned in Scripture. He is invoked for finding one’s future spouse, for healing, for travelers, and for the blind.

title: St Rita of Cascia Novena - Powerful Prayer for Impossible Causes
description: St Rita of Cascia was a wife and mother, then after her husband was killed, a nun. Pray this novena for impossible causes, marital problems, and abuse.

title: St Robert Bellarmine Novena
description: St. Robert Bellarmine was an Italian Jesuit Bishop and Doctor of the Church. Pray this novena for canonists, canon lawyers, catechists, catechumens, and the Archdiocese of Cincinnati.

title: St Rocco Novena Against Plagues
description: St Rocco was a fourteenth century Catholic saint and confessor who is invoked against the plague. Pray this coronavirus novena for God to stop it.

title: St. Rose of Lima Novena
description: St Rose of Lima was a Third Order Dominican renowned for her piety and chastity. Pray this novena for chastity, against vanity, for embroiderers, sewing lace, gardeners and florists, Latin America, people ridiculed or misunderstood for their piety, for the resolution of family quarrels, indigenous peoples of the Americas, Peru, Philippines, India, Santa Rosa California, Trinidad and Tobago, and Lima.

title: St Rose Philippine Duchesne Novena
description: St Rose Philippine Duchesne was a French nun of the Society of the Sacred Heart. Pray this novena for perseverance amid adversity and for Diocese of Springfield-Cape Girardeau.

title: St Scholastica Novena
description: St Scholastica was the twin sister of St Benedict and the foundress of the Benedictine nuns. Pray this novena for Benedictine women’s communities, schools, tests, books, reading, convulsive children, nuns, invoked against storms and rain.

title: St. Sebastian Novena
description: St Sebastian was a martyr in the third century under Diocletian. Pray this novena for soldiers, athletes, difficult trials, and those who desire a holy death.

title: St. Stephen Novena
description: St Stephen was a Deacon and the first Christian martyr of the Church. He is the patron saint of Deacons, altar servers, bricklayers, casket makers, and stonemasons.

title: St. Teresa of Avila Novena
description: St. Teresa was a Spanish Carmelite nun who reformed the Order and experienced mystical grace through prayer

title: St Teresa of the Andes Novena
description: St Teresa of Jesus of Los Andes was a Discalced Carmelite nun and the first canonized Chilean saint. Pray this novena for young people.

title: St. Therese of Lisieux Rose Novena - Little Flower Prayers
description: St. Thérèse, known as the Little Flower, was a French Discalced Carmelite nun who achieved great holiness through her Little Way of offering every small sacrifice to Jesus.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas Novena
description: Born in Italy in 1225, Thomas was gifted with an unparalleled intellect. He sought to enter the Dominican Order, but his family stopped him for some time by capturing him and holding him in their castle.

title: St Thomas Becket Novena
description: St Thomas Becket was an English Archbishop of Canterbury martyred in 1170. He is the patron saint of Exeter college, Oxford, Portsmouth, Arbroath Abbey, secular clergy, City of London, and Canterbury Cathedral.

title: St. Thomas More Novena
description: St Thomas More was an English lawyer and statesman who was martyred for his faith by King Henry VIII. Pray this novena for adopted children lawyers, civil servants, politicians, and difficult marriages.

title: St. Valentine Novena
description: St Valentine was a priest or bishop who lived in the third century, comforted persecuted Christians, and gave his life in martyrdom. He is the patron saint of engaged couples and marriages.

title: St Veronica Giuliani Novena
description: St Veronica Giuliani was an Italian Capuchin nun and mystic who lived in the 1600s and early 1700s. Pray this novena photographers, the poor, and the sick.

title: St Vincent de Paul Novena
description: St Vincent de Paul was a French Catholic priest who dedicated himself to serving the poor. Pray this novena for charities, horses, hospitals, leprosy, lost articles, prisoners, spiritual help, Saint Vincent de Paul Societies, and volunteers.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer Novena
description: St Vincent Ferrer was a Spanish Dominican priest and confessor renowned for his preaching and learning. He is the patron saint of Valencia, Spain, and of builders because he built up the Church.

title: Sts Cosmas and Damian Novena
description: Saints Cosmas and Damian were twin brothers born in Arabia in the 3rd century AD who became physicians. They are the patron saints of surgeons, physicians, identical twins, twins, dentists, protectors of children, barbers, pharmacists, veterinarians, orphanages, day-care centers, confectioners, against hernia, against the plague.

title: Saints Jacinta and Francisco Marto Fatima Seers Novena
description: Sts Jacinta and Francisco Marto, also known with their cousin Lucia dos Santos as the Fatima Seers, were poor children from Portugal to whom Our Lady of Fatima appeared in 1917. Pray this novena for bodily ills, captives, people ridiculed for their piety, prisoners, sick people, against sickness.

title: Sts. Joachim and Anne Novena
description: Saints Anne and Joachim are the parents of the Blessed Virgin Mary. Pray this novena for grandparents or to conceive a child.

title: Saints Louis and Zelie Martin Novena
description: Saints Louis and Zelie Martin were a French Catholic couple and the parents of five nuns, including St Thérèse of Lisieux. Pray this novena for illness, mental illness, marriage, parenting, and widowers.

title: Sts Perpetua and Felicity Novena
description: Sts Perpetua and Felicity were early Christian martyrs who lived in the second and early third centuries. Pray this novena for mothers, expectant mothers, widows, ranchers, and butchers.

title: Sts Philip and James Novena
description: Sts. Philip and James were two of the twelve Apostles called by Christ. Pray this novena for Uruguay, pharmacists, hatmakers, pastry chefs, and bakers.

title: Surrender Novena
description: Jesus Christ revealed the Surrender Novena to Fr. Dolingo Ruotolo, an Italian priest, to teach us to turn our troubles over to Him and surrender them to His will.

title: The Three Hail Marys Novena
description: St. Melchtilde beseeched Our Blessed Mother to assist her in her hour of death, and Our Lady appeared to her and said: I will, certainly. But I also want you to say three special Hail Marys to me every day.

title: Mary, the Virgin of Revelation Novena
description: An incredible, yet lesser known, novena to Our Lady based on her appearance to a man who plotted to murder the Pope in the 1940s.

title: Visitation Novena
description: The Feast of the Visitation commemorates the visit of the Blessed Virgin Mary to her cousin St. Elizabeth, as described in the Gospel of Luke (Luke 1:39-56). Pray this novena for the pro-life cause, babies, to conceive a child, and for infertility.
